title: Communication Error (Indoor-Outdoor)
shortDescription: Communication failure between indoor and outdoor units.
fullDescription: Error E0:00 on Fujitsu mini-splits indicates a total communication breakdown between the indoor and outdoor units. Check interconnecting wiring, connectors, and control boards.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the 3-wire or 4-wire communication cable between indoor and outdoor units for damage.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and firmly reseat all wire connectors at both indoor and outdoor control boards.

fixSteps.title: Test Continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test continuity on each communication wire end-to-end.

whenToCallPro: If wiring checks out but error persists — likely a PCB issue.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E0:00 mean?
faq.a: Communication failure between indoor and outdoor units.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Communication Error (Remote Controller)
shortDescription: The indoor unit cannot communicate with the wired or wireless remote.
fullDescription: Error E1:00 indicates the remote controller is not communicating with the indoor unit. For wireless remotes, this is often battery-related. For wired remotes, check the communication cable.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Replace Batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Install fresh batteries in the wireless remote.

fixSteps.title: Test IR Signal
fixSteps.instruction: Point phone camera at remote and press button — look for IR flash.

fixSteps.title: Check Wired Remote Cable
fixSteps.instruction: For wired remotes, check the 2-wire cable between remote and indoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Try Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Remove batteries, hold any button for 10 seconds, reinstall batteries.

whenToCallPro: If a new remote or new batteries do not fix the issue.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:00 mean?
faq.a: The indoor unit cannot communicate with the wired or wireless remote.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated easy. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: High Pressure Trip
shortDescription: The high-pressure protection switch has tripped on the outdoor unit.
fullDescription: Error E3:00 indicates the refrigerant pressure in the outdoor unit has exceeded safe limits. This is a safety shutdown to protect the compressor and refrigerant circuit.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Clean Condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the outdoor coil with a garden hose from inside out.

fixSteps.title: Clear Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris, plants, and objects within 24 inches of the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Check Outdoor Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the fan spins freely and starts when the unit powers on.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then restart.

whenToCallPro: If error returns after cleaning and ensuring proper airflow.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E3:00 mean?
faq.a: The high-pressure protection switch has tripped on the outdoor unit.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated easy. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Low Pressure Trip
shortDescription: The low-pressure protection has activated due to insufficient refrigerant pressure.
fullDescription: Error E4:00 indicates refrigerant pressure has dropped below the safe operating threshold. This commonly indicates a refrigerant leak or restriction in the system.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Check Filters
fixSteps.instruction: Clean indoor air filters — restricted airflow can mimic low pressure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Lines
fixSteps.instruction: Look for oil stains on refrigerant fittings indicating a leak.

fixSteps.title: Feel Lines
fixSteps.instruction: In cooling mode, the large suction line should be cool. If warm, refrigerant is low.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: Leak detection and refrigerant charging requires certified technician.

whenToCallPro: Immediately — low pressure almost always means a leak needing professional repair.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E4:00 mean?
faq.a: The low-pressure protection has activated due to insufficient refrigerant pressure.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated professional. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Compressor Overcurrent
shortDescription: The compressor is drawing excessive electrical current.
fullDescription: Error E5:00 means the compressor motor is pulling more current than the protection threshold allows. This could indicate a mechanical or electrical issue with the compressor.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Check Voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure supply voltage at outdoor disconnect — should be within 10% of nameplate.

fixSteps.title: Check Connections
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten all electrical connections at the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle the system and observe if the compressor starts.

fixSteps.title: Professional Diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: Compressor amperage and winding resistance tests require a technician.

whenToCallPro: If voltage is normal but compressor still trips on overcurrent.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E5:00 mean?
faq.a: The compressor is drawing excessive electrical current.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated professional. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Indoor Coil Freeze Protection
shortDescription: The indoor coil temperature has dropped to freezing levels.
fullDescription: Error E6:00 activates freeze protection when the indoor evaporator coil temperature drops below safe limits during cooling. This prevents ice formation that can damage the system.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Stop System
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off cooling and switch to fan-only to thaw the coil.

fixSteps.title: Clean Filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and wash all indoor air filters.

fixSteps.title: Check Return Air
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure furniture and curtains are not blocking the indoor unit intake.

fixSteps.title: Monitor
fixSteps.instruction: Restart cooling after thaw and monitor — if icing recurs, call a pro.

whenToCallPro: If icing returns after cleaning filters — likely a refrigerant issue.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E6:00 mean?
faq.a: The indoor coil temperature has dropped to freezing levels.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated easy. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Outdoor Fan Motor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor unit fan motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error E7:00 indicates the outdoor fan motor has failed or its speed feedback signal is not being received by the control board.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Visual Check
fixSteps.instruction: Look through the outdoor unit grille to see if the fan blade is jammed by debris.

fixSteps.title: Spin Test
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try to spin the fan blade by hand — it should rotate freely.

fixSteps.title: Check Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Open the electrical panel and reseat the fan motor connector.

fixSteps.title: Test Motor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure motor winding resistance to check for open or shorted windings.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor does not spin freely or has abnormal resistance readings.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E7:00 mean?
faq.a: The outdoor unit fan motor is not operating correctly.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Drain Overflow
shortDescription: The condensate drain pan has overflowed or the float switch has tripped.
fullDescription: Error E9:00 indicates the condensate drainage system has detected an overflow. The indoor unit shuts down to prevent water damage to the structure.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Use a wet-dry vacuum on the drain outlet to clear the clog.

fixSteps.title: Flush Line
fixSteps.instruction: Pour warm water with vinegar through the drain line.

fixSteps.title: Check Pump
fixSteps.instruction: If a condensate pump is installed, verify the float switch moves and pump activates.

fixSteps.title: Verify Flow
fixSteps.instruction: Pour water into the drain pan and confirm it drains properly.

whenToCallPro: If drain is clear but error continues recurring.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E9:00 mean?
faq.a: The condensate drain pan has overflowed or the float switch has tripped.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated easy. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Intake Thermistor Fault (Indoor)
shortDescription: The indoor unit intake air temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E1:01 indicates the room intake air thermistor is reading out of range or is disconnected. This sensor is essential for temperature regulation.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.
symptoms: System runs continuously or not at all

fixSteps.title: Access Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit front cover and locate the intake thermistor.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Connector
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and reconnect the thermistor plug on the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Test Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure thermistor resistance — approximately 10kΩ at 25°C.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Install a new OEM thermistor if resistance is out of specification.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not resolve the error.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:01 mean?
faq.a: The indoor unit intake air temperature sensor has failed.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Pipe Thermistor Fault (Indoor)
shortDescription: The indoor coil pipe temperature sensor is faulty.
fullDescription: Error E1:02 indicates the indoor evaporator pipe thermistor has failed or is detached from the coil. This sensor monitors coil temperature for defrost and freeze protection.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Reattach Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Clip the sensor back onto the copper pipe and cover with insulation tape.

fixSteps.title: Check Wire
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the thermistor wire for cuts or damage.

fixSteps.title: Test Resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance and compare to the service manual temperature chart.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Install new pipe thermistor if resistance is abnormal.

whenToCallPro: If icing continues after sensor replacement.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:02 mean?
faq.a: The indoor coil pipe temperature sensor is faulty.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Outdoor Pipe Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor coil temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E1:03 indicates the outdoor coil pipe thermistor is reading out of range. This sensor is critical for defrost control and high-pressure protection.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.
symptoms: Outdoor coil ices up in heat mode

fixSteps.title: Locate Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the coil thermistor on the outdoor unit — clipped to the coil piping.

fixSteps.title: Reattach
fixSteps.instruction: Secure the sensor to the pipe with a clip and cover with insulation.

fixSteps.title: Test
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance compared to outdoor temperature using the service chart.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Install OEM replacement sensor if readings are abnormal.

whenToCallPro: If defrost issues persist after sensor replacement.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:03 mean?
faq.a: The outdoor coil temperature sensor has failed.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Outdoor Ambient Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor ambient air temperature sensor is faulty.
fullDescription: Error E1:04 means the outdoor ambient temperature thermistor is reading abnormally. This sensor assists with capacity control and operating range management.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.
symptoms: System may not start in extreme temperatures

fixSteps.title: Find Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the ambient thermistor on the outdoor unit — typically mounted in the air intake path.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat the connector on the outdoor PCB.

fixSteps.title: Test
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance — compare to published temperature-resistance values.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Swap the thermistor with a matching OEM part.

whenToCallPro: If the replacement sensor does not resolve the error.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:04 mean?
faq.a: The outdoor ambient air temperature sensor is faulty.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Discharge Temperature Thermistor Fault
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error E1:05 indicates the compressor discharge pipe thermistor is reading out of range. This sensor protects the compressor from overheating.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Find Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the discharge thermistor strapped to the compressor discharge pipe.

fixSteps.title: Check Strap
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the sensor is tight against the pipe — loose sensors read inaccurately.

fixSteps.title: Test
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance with compressor off and compare to specification.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Install new thermistor and secure with insulation and a stainless steel strap.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement does not resolve or compressor has other issues.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E1:05 mean?
faq.a: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has failed.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated intermediate. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: Inverter Board Error
shortDescription: The outdoor unit inverter board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: Error E2:00 indicates a critical failure of the inverter power module or board in the outdoor unit. The inverter board controls compressor speed for variable-capacity operation.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.
symptoms: Indoor fan runs but no heating/cooling

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 10 minutes, then restore.

fixSteps.title: Check Voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify stable power supply to the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Visual Inspection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the electrical panel and look for burnt components or discoloration.

fixSteps.title: Professional Repair
fixSteps.instruction: Inverter board replacement requires factory parts and trained technician.

whenToCallPro: After power cycling fails — this is a professional-only repair.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error E2:00 mean?
faq.a: The outdoor unit inverter board has detected an internal fault.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated professional. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.

title: EEPROM Memory Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM has a read or write fault.
fullDescription: Error EE:EE indicates the non-volatile memory on the indoor or outdoor PCB has become corrupted or cannot be accessed. Configuration data may be lost.
safetyWarning: Mini-split systems use high-pressure refrigerant. Do not open refrigerant lines without EPA certification and proper tools.

fixSteps.title: Full Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 10 minutes, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Reconfigure
fixSteps.instruction: Re-enter your preferred temperature and mode settings.

fixSteps.title: Monitor
fixSteps.instruction: Watch for the error to recur over the next 48 hours.

fixSteps.title: Board Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If error persists, the PCB with the EEPROM needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error returns after a thorough power cycle.

faq.q: What does Fujitsu error EE:EE mean?
faq.a: The control board EEPROM has a read or write fault.

faq.q: Is this a common Fujitsu error?
faq.a: This is a frequently reported error code on Fujitsu mini-split systems.

faq.q: Can I fix this myself?
faq.a: DIY difficulty is rated easy. Professional help may be needed for component replacement.
